*** 4 FATAL, 72 INJ *** A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN WESTFIELD AT 417 PM. THE TORNADO TRAVELED TO THE EAST AND ENDED AT 527 PM IN CHARLTON. THE RATING OF THE TORNADO WAS DETERMINED TO BE EF3 WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 160 MPH. THE TORNADO HAD A NEAR CONTINUOUS PATH FROM WESTFIELD TO CHARLTON. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 39 MILES AND THE MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS ONE HALF MILE. FATALITIES AND INJURIES WERE TABULATED BY THE MASSACHUSETTS EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT AGENCY.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN THE TOWN OF WILBRAHAM FROM 632 PM UNTIL 640 PM. THE TORNADO WAS RATED AS AN EF1 WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 90 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 3.6 MILES AND THE MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 200 YARDS. THERE WERE ZERO FATALITIES OR INJURIES.
TWO FEET OF STREET FLOODING AT THE INTERSECTION OF MILLBURY AVENUE AND WHEELOCK STREET.
ROUTE 122 AND 203 INTERCHANGE FLOODED WITH 3 FEET OF WATER AT THE INTERSECTION.

A SHORT LIVED TORNADO MOVED THROUGH THE NORTHERN PORTION OF BRIMFIELD BETWEEN 642 PM TO 657 PM. THE RATING WAS DETERMINED TO BE EF1 WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 90 MPH. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 1.3 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 100 YARDS.THERE WERE NO INJURIES OR FATALITIES WITH THIS TORNADO.

LAHEY CLINIC HAS 4 TO 6 FEET OF WATER IN THE BASEMENT IN ADDITION TO SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER IN THE LOWER HALLWAYS OF THE CLINIC. OIL COMING OUT OF FLOODED BASEMENT ON LYNNFIELD STREET. WATER RESCUE IN HOME ON TUMELTY STREET.

FLOODING CLOSURES...RTE 128 S FROM 114 LOWELL ST. LAKE ST TO RTE 1 S...TRUCK IN SINK HOLE. ALSO BRIMBALL ST TO RTE 128 N BARELY PASSABLE.
CREEK BEHIND PEABODY ACCESS TELEVISION FLOODING THE BACK PARKING LOT. FOSTER ST UNDER AT LEAST 2 FEET OF WATER. ENDICOTT ST STILL FLOODED UP TO 2 FEET. 100 BASEMENTS BEING PUMPED OUT.
AN ESTIMATED 100 HOMES AND 40 BUSINESSES EXPERIENCED DAMAGE FROM FLOODING. FIRST FLOOR OF THE POLICE DEPARTMENT WAS FLOODED EXACERBATED BY A PUMP FAILURE.
1ST FLOOR BUSINESSES AT CUMMINGS CENTER SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT FLOOD DAMAGE. THE DPW GARAGE WAS FLOODED DAMAGING POLICE CARS AND OTHER CITY VEHICLES.
